post.description:
Mint a one-shot, 60-second ticket for opening a WebSocket connection.

The ticket can be consumed exactly once via the `?ticket=` query param on
the /chat/ws endpoint. Use this instead of passing the access token in the
URL — URLs can leak via browser history, proxies, and access logs.

schema.title: Response Get Activity Profile Activity Get
Response Get Achievements Profile Achievements Get

get.description:
Get approximate online viewer count for a problem's discussion.

Accepts both numeric problem ID and problem slug.
Unauthenticated access is allowed.

List all discussion posts for a problem.

Accepts both numeric problem ID and problem slug.
Unauthenticated users can read; auth state determines computed fields
(is_upvoted, my_reactions).

post.description: Create a new discussion post (authenticated users only).

patch.description:
Update a discussion post (owner only, within 15 min).

Only the author can edit their own posts within 15 minutes of creation.

post.description:
Toggle upvote on a discussion post (idempotent).

Returns { "is_upvoted": bool }

Add a reply to a discussion post.

Returns the newly created reply as ReplyOut.

schema.title: Response Create Reply Problems  Problem Id  Discussions  Post Id  Replies Post

post.description:
Toggle upvote on a reply (idempotent).

Returns { "is_upvoted": bool }

Mark/unmark a reply as accepted (post owner only).

Only the original post author can mark answers as accepted.
Calling again on the same reply will unmark it.

Returns { "is_accepted": bool }
Execute code without persisting. Used for the 'Run' button in the IDE.
Submit code for grading; persists a row and awards XP on acceptance.

get.description: Get the authenticated user's submission history.

schema.title: Response Get My Submissions Submissions My Submissions Get

post.description:
Broadcast a typing indicator to the other participant.

Clients fire this at most once per keystroke burst (debounced on the
frontend).  The server fans it out over WebSocket with no persistence.

schema.title: Response Search Users Chat Users Search Get

post.description: Mark a course as completed by the authenticated user.
Self-enroll in a course. Idempotent: returns existing if already enrolled.

put.description:
Update progress for a lesson. Auto-enrolls in the course if needed.

Use `:path` so lesson_id can contain dots (e.g. 'recsys.m01.l03').

Body fields are all optional — pass only what you want to change:
  - status: 'in_progress' | 'completed'
  - last_position: any string the frontend understands
  - time_delta_seconds: increment to add (capped at 600 server-side)

get.description:
One-shot summary: per-course aggregates + 'continue learning' shortcut.

Designed to power the home dashboard with a single network call.
Lightweight badge endpoint — call this every 30s from the frontend.

post.description:
Broadcast a notification to every active user.

Body should be a JSON object with at minimum a `title` and `body` so the
frontend can render it. Example:
  {"title": "New course: RAG Evaluation", "body": "Just shipped!", "url": "/courses/rag-eval"}

get.description:
Returns whether the job board is unlocked for the current user,
along with progress percentages for problems and courses.
Unlocked when both problems and course lessons are >= 75% complete.

The frontend calls this on load to decide which UI to unlock.

Staff/admins always read as fully unlocked.

What MIRA knows about the user's course position — drives course-aware
tutoring and 'next lesson' style recommendations.

post.description:
Answer feedback -> learning signal. Maps ratings to learning events that
nudge style selection + mastery.

Records real mastery evidence: did the student answer correctly, did they
use a hint. This is what makes mastery evidence-based, not depth-based.

get.description: Readiness probe — verifies DB and Redis are reachable.

Prometheus exposition endpoint.

Restrict access at the ALB / security-group level in production —
the metrics body is not authenticated here.
